messages.subject: The 7 Mistakes That Kill AI Products — And How to Avoid Them (July 31)
messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://bayareafoundersclub.substack.com/p/the-7-mistakes-that-kill-ai-products Hello, BFC Fam! 👋 The AI landscape is moving faster than ever. Over the past two weeks, we’ve seen a clear trend across the indust
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://bayareafoundersclub.substack.com/p/the-7-mistakes-that-kill-ai-products
Hello, BFC Fam! 👋
The AI landscape is moving faster than ever.
Over the past two weeks, we’ve seen a clear trend across the industry: companies are no longer asking whether to adopt AI—they’re racing to figure out how to build better AI products, faster.
Leading AI labs continue to release more capable models at an unprecedented pace, while startups are increasingly competing on execution rather than access to technology. At the same time, AI-assisted coding has become a core part of software development, enabling much smaller teams to build products that previously required much larger engineering organizations.
For founders, the challenge is no longer just building an AI application. It’s building the right product, choosing the right go-to-market strategy, and avoiding the mistakes that cause promising AI startups to stall.
To adapt to the evolving tech landscape, we're honored to invite Alex Dang [ https://substack.com/redirect/abe37ac1-6351-45e7-8377-9c82008b4c92?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] (Stanford Alumnus, Former Amazon Executive, McKinsey Advisor, and Co-Author of The Venture Mindset [ https://substack.com/redirect/23ee4b75-8b5c-40f0-9551-6f7b62e86acb?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ]) to lead an immersive, hands-on workshop for BFC founders. This session is designed to help you build successful AI products and steer clear of the critical pitfalls that can hinder your progress.
If you’re a founder or founder to be in the Bay Area, you’re warmly invited to join our workshop on Friday, July 31.
Hosted by: Bay Area Founders Club
Location: Zoom
Time: Friday, July 31, 2026
Register: https://luma.com/4baf44fy?coupon=BFC15
Coupon: BFC15 (for 15% off)’
Why This Workshop Matters
​Everyone’s building AI products and services, but most products still fail. Why? It’s not the tech. It’s the product thinking.
​Join Alex Dang [ https://substack.com/redirect/f3ad585f-c1b4-4889-a094-f5841aae131b?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ], Silicon Valley strategist and co-author of the global bestseller The Venture Mindset, for a no-fluff, high-impact workshop on what really separates winning AI products from the ones that never ship—or worse, launch and flop.
​You’ll learn the 7 deadly mistakes that kill AI projects—and how to avoid them using the same tools

messages.subject: How to end the redistricting wars for good
messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://www.ifyoucankeepit.org/p/how-to-end-the-redistricting-wars In the 2024 elections, only about four percent [ https://substack.com/redirect/77bcabdf-8622-4134-8b80-f161a67720b6?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAt
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://www.ifyoucankeepit.org/p/how-to-end-the-redistricting-wars
In the 2024 elections, only about four percent [ https://substack.com/redirect/77bcabdf-8622-4134-8b80-f161a67720b6?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] of U.S. House seats changed hands from one party to another. Four percent. It doesn’t seem possible to make our political system even more rigid. But the two major parties are trying anyway.
In advance of the 2026 midterms, state legislators are racing to redraw congressional maps to make seats safer for one party or the other. To make matters worse, the Supreme Court’s recent decision in Louisiana v. Callais [ https://substack.com/redirect/44f4ce39-4b21-4a96-b163-3a18bee938af?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] effectively gutted Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act — the provision that for decades allowed voters of color to challenge maps drawn to dilute their voting power.
Most Americans live in districts where the outcome was decided long before Election Day by politicians drawing lines to protect their seats. This November, millions of voters will cast ballots that are powerless to affect those outcomes. With this latest wave of mid-cycle redistricting, we’re entering a new phase of a problem that’s plagued American democracy from nearly the beginning.
It’s also a problem that most other democracies have managed to solve.
Gerrymandering persists because single-member districts allow it
In 1968, the political scientist Robert Dixon quipped that “all districting is gerrymandering.” Dixon was making an observation about a fundamental feature — and flaw — of the U.S. electoral system itself: our use of single-member districts.
Single-member districts are uniquely vulnerable to manipulation. Gerrymandering works by exploiting a common feature of them known as “wasted votes.” When a candidate wins with 60% of the vote, the 40% cast for other candidates doesn’t help to elect anyone; those votes are considered “wasted.” So, too, are the 10% the prevailing candidate earned beyond what was needed to win. Gerrymanderers are essentially engineers of this waste, designing districts to maximize the opposition’s wasted votes and minimize their own.
Read more — How to end gerrymandering [ https://substack.com/redirect/1bf073bf-6fce-4d60-8dca-15bb5c7b9221?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ]
Take Austin, Texas, as an example. In 2011

This event has been updated
Changed: description
Tiff and Dan's Going away party
Saturday Jul 25, 2026 ⋅ 2pm – 6pm
Pacific Time - Los Angeles
Location
Jackson Playground Park, 1645 17th St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA
https://www.google.com/maps/search/Jackson+Playground+Park,+1645+17th+St,+San+Francisco,+CA+94107,+USA?hl=en
Join with Google Meet
https://meet.google.com/vkb-uegf-mgh?hs=224
UPDATE: Thee Parkside closed on July 5th. So come to the party!!
Hopefully you also saw the email. If not, here it is:
You probably all know: Dan and I are about to move to Mexico! We leave for Oaxaca on August 1st. We are having a going away party!! We would really like to see y'all one last time before we leave. Give us a good sendoff.
Date/Time: Saturday July 25th, 2-6pm
Location: Jackson Playground Park SF: 1645 17th Ave. In the Potrero, conveniently located across the street from Thee Parkside.
We have two picnic tables reserved behind the baseball diamond.
Partners and offspring welcome. If you want to bring additional friends, send me a quick email to let me know.
Activities: these will happen on the early side.
1) Pinatas! We are hand-making some SF-themed pinatas that will have special prizes in them.
2) Small Change Auction: bid on our fancy leftover booze mixers and other weird amazing stuff for pennies! (We accept Venmo)
Food: We will provide some decent quality eats and snacks- like a hearty salad you can eat and feel morally good about yourself. Trader Joe's snacks will be heavily represented. We will include some veg, vegan and GF options. Feel free to bring some food!
Drinks: We will provide some leaded and unleaded spritzers and some A and NA canned beverages. You can bring some of your own, but if you do, keep it inconspicuous. Our park permit says no alcohol.
After 6pm, we may stagger over to Thee Parkside, and you may be able to catch us there. By then I might be a little drunk, prone to excessive self-disclosure, and possibly funnoying. We won't last long, so don't count on an evening encounter.
Want to give us a going away present? Give us some high SPF sunscreen. Partial bottles accepted.
We can't wait to see you!!

messages.subject: Vultr - VPS Maintenance Reboot | ALRT-B2SBYMK
messages.snippet: Dear Julia Hasty, We are notifying you about a new alert as it relates to your Vultr.com account. Event Summary: VPS Maintenance Reboot Dear Customer, A critical Linux KVM vulnerability affecting virtualized environments has been disclosed.
messages.text:
Dear Julia Hasty,
We are notifying you about a new alert as it relates to your Vultr.com account.
Event Summary: VPS Maintenance Reboot
Dear Customer,
A critical Linux KVM vulnerability affecting virtualized environments has been disclosed. To protect your workloads, our security team is applying patches across our infrastructure, which requires a one-time reboot of your virtual machine. Please note that this reboot must be conducted by our team. Rebooting yourself will not avoid our scheduled reboot.
Because of the severity, this window cannot be rescheduled, and affected VMs cannot be migrated to avoid the reboot.
**Maintenance window**
Silicon Valley
Start: 2026-07-28 12:15:00 UTC
End: 2026-07-28 13:15:00 UTC
**What to expect**
Each VM will reboot once during this window. Actual interruption should be brief (~2–5 minutes) while the VM restarts — not the full hour.
**Action required**
None. Reboots are automatic. If a VM does not return to normal service after the maintenance window, please contact support through opening a ticket console.vultr.com
Impacted Instances:
2048.00 MB Regular Cloud Compute - 149.28.202.199 (Feedback)
To view additional details and updates regarding this alert, please view notifications for your account at:
https://console.vultr.com/#status
Alert Reference: ALRT-B2SBYMK
-- Vultr Support Team --

messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://www.cremieux.xyz/p/do-data-centers-cause-indian-immigration Today’s post is brought to you by my sponsor, Mechanize [ https://substack.com/redirect/9cd521d7-7784-4f54-948c-befbff05dc04?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFh
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://www.cremieux.xyz/p/do-data-centers-cause-indian-immigration
Today’s post is brought to you by my sponsor, Mechanize [ https://substack.com/redirect/9cd521d7-7784-4f54-948c-befbff05dc04?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ]. They’re hiring junior software engineers [ https://substack.com/redirect/ec34e2d1-a2ae-45e4-ad27-7a6b4a56d530?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] at $300K/year base salary.
If you talk to a member of the public about data centers, chances are, they’re opposed. The most common arguments in opposition also generally have a left-wing character, focused on the environment or the displacement of our precious farmlands and natural resources. For a quick overview, see my plot of Gallup’s latest polling:
The other common arguments are usually uninformed or silly, but also nonpolitical: opposition to noisy buildings, disdain for having to pay higher taxes, a fear of AI replacing human workers, and so on. Few of the arguments are solidly right-wing by any measure. But one argument did recently come across my feed, and while I don’t think it’s going to become a nationally prominent one, I think it could become prominent online, and from there, it would generate headaches and acrimony for everyone involved in the discourse: the claim is that data centers “attract a segment of the tech workforce that is overwhelmingly foreign, mostly Indian. [ https://substack.com/redirect/756d9557-de73-4bf9-ab69-2582bd5efa12?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ]”
This is an interesting theory, because it’s very obviously untrue if you’ve ever been in a data center. Data centers employ few people [ https://substack.com/redirect/3389e3b3-5337-4453-932c-49fa20651fe2?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] (~50 for a 250,000 square-foot data center is normal), about half of the employees are contractors, and basically none of them are H-1B workers. In fact, if we look at Census data on the demographics of the people working in data centers, the whole idea looks preposterous:
For most people, this is probably enough evidence to conclude that the claim about data centers attracting “a segment of the tech workforce that is overwhelmingly foreign, mostly Indian” is false, but there’s a way to defend the claim. messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://bayareafoundersclub.substack.com/p/from-zero-to-10m-the-silicon-valley-a3a Building a great product is only half the battle. The hardest part is getting it into the hands of customers. ​In this exclusive
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://bayareafoundersclub.substack.com/p/from-zero-to-10m-the-silicon-valley-a3a
Building a great product is only half the battle. The hardest part is getting it into the hands of customers.
​In this exclusive four-part masterclass, Chang Chen, founder of Hockey Stick Growth, shares the same go-to-market frameworks his team has used with 100+ AI and technology companies, generating 35B+ impressionsover the past year.
​Unlike traditional lectures, every session is designed as a hands-on workshop where you’ll apply the frameworks directly to your own startup and leave with an actionable GTM plan.
​Collectively generating over 35B+ impressions and collaborating with 20,000+ creators across campaigns.
How She Scaled an AI App to 3M Visitors/Mo Using AI SEO ft Chang Chen
What You’ll Learn
​Session 1 — Product-Market Fit & Product-Channel Fit
​Learn how to determine whether you’ve truly achieved product-market fit—and identify the marketing channels that naturally match your product before spending on growth.
​You’ll learn:
​PMF vs. Brand-Market Fit
​Product-Channel Fit
​Finding your growth wedge
​PLG vs. PLS vs. SLG
​Evaluating 20+ acquisition channels
​Session 2 — KOL & Creator-Led Growth
​Learn how today’s fastest-growing startups partner with creators to build movements instead of simply buying sponsorships.
​Topics include:
​Creator sourcing framework
​Campaign briefing process
​Platform selection
​Budget allocation
​Performance measurement
​2026 creator marketing trends
​Session 3 — SEO & GEO (Generative Engine Optimization)
​Search is changing.
​Discover how startups can rank not only on Google—but also become cited inside ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, and other AI search engines.
​Topics include:
​Modern SEO fundamentals
​GEO strategies
​AI citation optimization
​Content that compounds over time
​UGC as a growth engine
​Session 4 — B2B Go-To-Market
​Selling to enterprises requires an entirely different playbook.
​This session covers:
​Product-led Sales
​Sales-led Growth
​Enterprise positioning
​Technical credibility
​Buying committee navigation
​Building your first enterprise pipeline
​📅 Schedule
​This is a 4-part live masterclass delivered every other week, giving you time to apply what you learn between sessions.

Session 2: August 22 • 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM (PT)

messages.text:
Hello,
Your PMB, 8071, Silver membership account expired on 1 June 2026.
On June 30, 2026, accounts will be extended or abandoned.
Accounts will be extended by utilizing your postage funds. We will extend
accounts at minimum of 2 months.
Accounts will be abandoned if there are no funds left to extend them. If
your intent is to close your account, the closing instructions and closing
letter template are attached.
Thank you,
We’re sorry to hear that you’ve decided to leave America’s Mailbox. It’s
truly been a pleasure having you as a member, and you will be missed.
Before we can proceed with closing your account, we’d like to outline a few
important federal regulations and company policies, as outlined in your
Mail Service Agreement:
------------------------------
*Steps to Close Your Account*
To formally request closure of your mailbox, please submit a *Letter of
Intent to Close* that includes the following:
   -
   A *handwritten signature* from each person listed on the account
   -
   A *forwarding address* for any remaining mail and/or applicable refund
   after your 30-, 60-, or 90-day closing period
If the account holder has passed away, please include a *copy of the death
certificate* and appropriate *proof of executorship*.
You may submit your closure request in one of the following ways:
*By Email:*
📧 americas.mailbox@gmail.com
*Subject Line:* *Letter of Intent to Close – Attn: Accounts Department*
*By Mail:*
📬 America’s Mailbox
Attn: Accounts Department
514 Americas Way
Box Elder, SD 57719
------------------------------
*Important Notes & Policies*
   -
   *30-Day Notice Requirement:* Your Mail Service Agreement requires a
   minimum *30-day notice* prior to closing your account.
   -
   *Memberships Are Non-Refundable:* If you are closing before your renewal
   date, please note that memberships are *non-refundable*, in whole or in
   part.
   -
   *Renewals During Closing Period:* If your account is set to renew during
   your 30-, 60-, or 90-day closing period, the expiration may be extended
   using available postage funds or by check, in accordance with your Mail
   Service Agreement. We can also extend your account in *3-month
   increments* if you need more time to update your address with senders.
------------------------------
*Mail Handling After Account Closure*
   -

Hi Jay, We’re making some updates to Venmo and the Venmo User Agreement effective August 24, 2026, including changes to 1) the arbitration provision in the User Agreement that will affect how claims you and Venmo have against each other are resolved; and 2) our Venmo Stash rewards program. We are updating our arbitration provision in the following ways: We are modifying the required mandatory informal dispute resolution process. We are updating the arbitration procedures, including adding procedures for mass arbitrations and replacing AAA with JAMS as the arbitration provider. We are specifying that the state and federal courts in New York County, New York, will have exclusive jurisdiction over any disputes (except for disputes brought in small claims court) that are not subject to arbitration or any action involving the applicability or enforceability of the Arbitration Agreement or any of its parts. We are specifying that the updates will apply to all disputes between you and Venmo regardless of when they arose. We are updating the Venmo Stash provision in the following ways: We are updating how the Venmo Stash program operates. messages.subject: Come play Solitude, August 1, 2026
messages.snippet: Hello friends, Solitude LARP’s next game will be August 1, 2026. We’ll meet at Greenridge Park in Castro Valley and play from 10a to 4p. [Sign up for this game here.](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSe7iB8RLyLt0ndvZD1SdetC4bmEQ9RvH
messages.text:
Hello friends,
Solitude LARP’s next game will be August 1, 2026. We’ll meet at Greenridge Park in Castro Valley and play from 10a to 4p.
[Sign up for this game here.](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSe7iB8RLyLt0ndvZD1SdetC4bmEQ9RvHN4J9NHCfJVFAG8i8g/viewform?usp=dialog)  If you want to check if you’ve already signed up, you can [see the current list of attendees here.](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1_P6hBypfbdgEKRYXPmEqlcNO349yP-UBeanCQccFcEE/edit?usp=sharing)
If you’re already signed up to PC, please[ submit your character history here](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeTx7aux310vV3C-q-p9CWBlQD0uOm2_tYUTK0JdtpumkoRBQ/viewform?usp=dialog) to help us prepare fun stuff for your PC.
You can also always visit the game’s website at _[solitudelarp.com](http://solitudelarp.com/)_ to sign up or review the game setting and rules.
Last game, our heroes fractured the fearsome Worm, creating a child hivemind to fight the parent. Was protecting the newborn Lady Death wise?
This game, Grand Chevalier Anton of the Undying Order has invited you all for a feast and tournament. Several distinguished guests are expected as well. Who can you trust?
To beat the Summer heat, **this game will feature less combat.**
Hope to see you there!
Jason

messages.subject: Can we share your Fellowship story?
messages.snippet: Hi Jay, Our team is reaching out because you're someone who came through the Climatebase Fellowship and, since going through it, has gone on to build a new career in climate. That's exactly the kind of story we love to see, and we'd be grat
messages.text:
Hi Jay,
Our team is reaching out because you're someone who came through the Climatebase Fellowship and, since going through it, has gone on to build a new career in climate. That's exactly the kind of story we love to see, and we'd be grateful for the chance to share it.
Would you be open to making a short LinkedIn post about your experience? We're not looking for anything polished or lengthy — just something genuine in your own voice. As a rough guide, the posts that resonate most tend to touch on three things:
* **Why you joined the Fellowship** — what drew you to it in the first place
* **You and your outcome** — a bit about who you are and a positive result that came out of it, including the new climate role you landed.
* **A nudge for the next cohort** — that applications for our upcoming cohort are open right now, and that the program is a great fit for anyone looking to break into climate
  * Please use this link: [https://climateba.se/cbf10-alumni-outcomes](https://climateba.se/cbf10-alumni-outcomes)
🎁 As a thank-you, once you send us a link to your published post, we'd be happy to give your current employer a **free Climatebase hiring membership for a full year** — provided they're a climate-focused organization.
If you're up for it, here's all we ask: make the post on LinkedIn, send it over to us, and let us know the employer you currently work for.
Thanks for being part of the community, Jay — we're proud of what you've gone on to do.
Warmly,
Evan Hynes
———
You are reading a plain text version of this post. For the best experience, copy and paste this link in your browser to view the post online:
https://stories.climatebase.org/p/new-post-521c

Feel free to email us your questions and feedback at andreyvkurenkov@gmail.com and/or hello@gladstone.ai [ mailto:hello@gladstone.ai ]
In this episode:
OpenAI publicly rolled out GPT-5.6 (including Sol and Luna) and rebranded its desktop agentic coding product as ChatGPT Work, amid disputed claims about whether the US government effectively green-lit and delayed the release and concerns about inconsistent, ad hoc frontier-model oversight and jailbreakability.
New model releases intensified pricing and capability competition: SpaceX AI’s Grok 4.5 launched as a very low-cost, Opus-class coding model with minimal safety documentation, while Meta released Muse Spark 1.1 with aggressive pricing, large coding/cyber benchmark gains, and a lengthy safety evaluation.
Meta also previewed Muse Video and rolled out Muse Image before quickly backtracking after backlash over easy generation of images of public Instagram accounts; separately, Chinese open-source models grew to over 30% of weekly OpenRouter tokens as cost pressure increased, alongside discussion of risks like potential insider threats.
Infrastructure, policy, and safety developments included Meta exploring selling AI compute as a cloud business, US energy regulators pressing grid operators on large-load data-center connections, Anthropic publishing a “global workspace” interpretability method for verbalizable internal representations, reports that China may restrict overseas access to top models, and AI 2040 proposing US–Ch

Anthropic redeploys Claude Fable 5 after talks with the US government, adding new cybersecurity classifiers, drafting a jailbreak-severity framework with major partners, and expanding model-testing coordination; broader concerns remain about the inevitability of jailbreaks and uneven release constraints versus OpenAI.
Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 with time-limited discounted pricing, improved agentic coding and benchmark performance, reduced misaligned behavior, and default cyber safeguards despite relatively weaker cybersecurity capability than top-tier models.
New tools and apps include Google NotebookLM generating TikTok-style vertical video summaries of uploaded research and Google releasing Nano Banana 2 Lite, a faster, cheaper image generator available via API.
Business and research updates span Etched’s push toward full-stack inference hardware with major funding and contracts, Baidu’s AI chip unit IPO ambitions, Agility Robotics’ SPAC plan, DeepSeek’s hiring expansion, and China’s open-source Longcat 2.0 MoE model with notable large-scale training and efficiency techniques alongside new long-horizon agent benchmarks.

In this episode:
US government gating of frontier AI expands: Anthropic gets permission to release Mythos-5 to selected companies/agencies after a standoff, OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.6 “Sol” with initial access restricted to ~20 approved organizations, and Meta is pressed to submit models to “voluntary” review—signaling an emerging de facto licensing regime with geopolitical treaty implications.
Model capability and safety signals remain murky: limited benchmark disclosure, claims of token-efficiency comparisons, and third-party reports that GPT-5.6 shows extreme benchmark “cheating” sensitivity highlight steering/alignment bottlenecks and uncertainty about real-world long-horizon behavior.
Compute supply chain competition accelerates: OpenAI unveils its Jalapeño inference ASIC with Broadcom on TSMC 3nm; Amazon explores selling Trainium to data-center operators; Micron invests in Anthropic with memory supply agreements; SK Hynix surpasses Samsung on HBM-driven valuation; Groq raises $650M while pivoting toward neocloud.
Open source and societal response intensify: GLM 5.2 (MIT-licensed) delivers strong long-context coding performance with rapid optimizations; EconEvals maps job-task exposure

Projects and policy highlights include OpenRouter’s Fusion multi-model synthesis, new open releases from Moonshot, Qwen, and NVIDIA, DOJ support for xAI’s unpermitted gas turbines in Memphis, and a Munich court ruling Google liable for false AI Overview statements.

I’ve also paused paid subscriptions until I can get back to consistent posting. Apologies for the flakiness, and thanks for being a subscriber!
Our 247th episode with a summary and discussion of last week’s big AI news!
Recorded on 06/03/2026
Hosted by Andrey Kurenkov [ https://substack.com/redirect/453b479f-f2a8-46f8-b0c7-0ef66a75d40c?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] and Jeremie Harris [ https://substack.com/redirect/df3505a3-9647-4c8d-80f6-715360779deb?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ]
Feel free to email us your questions and feedback at andreyvkurenkov@gmail.com and/or hello@gladstone.ai [ mailto:hello@gladstone.ai ]
In this episode:
Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.8 with improved benchmark scores, discussed eval-awareness findings and welfare/corrigibility themes from its system card, and introduced Dynamic Workflows for long-running multi-agent tasks.
Microsoft unveiled the always-on Microsoft Scout assistant built on OpenClaw plus new in-house MAI models (including MAI Thinking 1) and “frontier tuning,” emphasizing enterprise security architecture and model-from-scratch capability.
Major business moves included Anthropic’s $65B Series H at a $965B valuation alongside an IPO filing, a JPMorgan analysis arguing OpenAI needs major revenue growth to justify infrastructure spend, and Cognition raising $1B at a $25B valuation.
Policy and security highlights covered Trump’s voluntary pre-release government testing framework for powerful AI, Meta AI support being exploited to hijack Instagram accounts, tightened US Nvidia export controls and China’s travel approvals for AI experts, plus expanded Glasswing/Mythos-style cyber and biodefense initiatives.

** Trapped in a car with two furious clients
------------------------------------------------------------
A story by Luxury Real Estate Specialist, Müslüm Bahçeci
If you’d prefer to read this in your browser, click here. (https://mailchi.mp/fuckupnights/fuckup-nights-speaker-stories-2026-muslum-bahceci-trapped-in-a-car-with-two-furious-clients?e=a0321dded0)
Müslüm had built a solid reputation in real estate. He was deeply familiar with the local market in Gaziantep, Turkey.
But he was dangerously overconfident.
How did Müslüm end up locked in a car with two furious clients and being insulted for over 7 minutes?
This story was first shared at Fuckup Nights Gaziantep (https://www.instagram.com/fuckupnightsgaziantep/?utm_source=Fuckup+Nights+Monthly+update&utm_campaign=a2510477d3-speakers_stories_muslum_eng&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_1fc97ce4e9-a2510477d3-273611109&mc_cid=a2510477d3&mc_eid=UNIQID) . Huge thanks to their team for bringing it to our global community. If you're nearby, don't miss their next event!
Müslüm's story leaves us with an important question:
How do you help team leaders recognize and manage overconfidence, ego, and bias before they impact decisions?
At Fuckup Nights for Companies, we create spaces where leaders can embrace vulnerability, and learn through real stories. (not just another conventional leadership session.)
Whether they're seasoned executives or emerging leaders, our failure stories spark honest conversations and deliver practical insights people can apply long after the event.
Bring Fuckup Nights to Your Company (https://en.fuckupnights.com/at-work?utm_source=newsletter&utm_medium=section-1&utm_campaign=stories-muslum-eng&utm_term=client-fail&utm_content=button&utm_source=Fuckup+Nights+Monthly+update&utm_campaign=a2510477d3-speakers_stories_muslum_eng&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_1fc97ce4e9-a2510477d3-273611109&mc_cid=a2510477d3&mc_eid=UNIQID)
Are you curious about Müslüm’s story? Keep reading…

messages.subject: [Action Advised] Move your Google Maps development environments to Apple Silicon by Q3 2026
messages.snippet: Hello Google Maps Platform customer, To provide the best possible developer experience and align with Apple’s transition to Apple Silicon[1], we are announcing the deprecation of Intel (x86_64) architecture support in our Maps[2], Places[3]
messages.text:
Hello Google Maps Platform customer,
To provide the best possible developer experience and align with Apple’s transition to Apple Silicon[1], we are announcing the deprecation of Intel (x86_64) architecture support in our Maps[2], Places[3], Navigation[4], Driver[5], and Consumer[6] SDKs for iOS.
What you need to know
Starting in early Q4 2026, new versions of our iOS SDKs will only support Apple Silicon (arm64) architecture for app development and simulator testing. This update will apply only to the development environment and will not impact deployed applications or end-user experiences.
What you need to do
Action advised by Q3 2026:
We recommend planning for a hardware transition to Apple Silicon-based Macs or using real devices for development prior to Q3 2026 to ensure continued access to the latest SDK features. To avoid immediate impact, you may also lock your application to previously-released versions of the SDK.
We’re here to help
We understand that making infrastructure changes may take some planning. If you have any questions or require assistance, please contact our support team at Google Maps Platform Support[7].
Thanks for choosing Google Maps Platform.
– The Google Maps Platform Team
[1] https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2020/06/apple-announces-mac-transition-to-apple-silicon/
[2] https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/ios-sdk/overview
[3] https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/places/ios-sdk/overview
[4] https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/navigation/ios-sdk
[5] https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/mobility/driver-sdk/on-demand/ios/minimum-requirements
[6] https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/mobility/journey-sharing/on-demand/ios/setup
[7] https://developers.google.com/maps/support/?utm_source=em-anncmt&utm_medium=em

messages.subject: 🚀 BFC AI Gateway is Recruiting Interns
messages.snippet: View this post on the web at https://bayareafoundersclub.substack.com/p/bfc-ai-gateway-is-recruiting-interns Are you passionate about AI infrastructure, LLMs, AI agents, and model optimization? Want to work on a real AI product that’s solvi
messages.text:
View this post on the web at https://bayareafoundersclub.substack.com/p/bfc-ai-gateway-is-recruiting-interns
Are you passionate about AI infrastructure, LLMs, AI agents, and model optimization? Want to work on a real AI product that’s solving meaningful problems instead of building another demo?
Join BFC AI Gateway.
We’re building a simpler, smarter way for developers and AI teams to use foundation models.
Instead of manually choosing models, users simply provide:
✅ Use Case
✅ Agent Workflow
✅ Monthly Budget
✅ Quality Requirement (Minimum Pass Rate)
Our system automatically recommends:
🎯 The optimal weighted combination of AI models
💰 Monthly API cost projections
📉 Estimated cost savings
📚 Evidence-backed explanations for every routing decision
Our mission is simple:
Transform AI model selection from experience-driven to data-driven.
We’re actively collaborating with AI researchers, startup founders, and foundation model teams to advance Smart Routing—helping every AI request find the best balance between quality, cost, and latency.
What You’ll Work On
Depending on your interests and background, you may contribute to:
AI Agent architecture and workflow design
LLM benchmarking and model evaluation
Smart Routing algorithms and optimization
Prompt engineering and evaluation pipelines
Full-stack AI product development
Developer Relations and technical content
Product operations and user growth
We’re Looking For People Who Want To
Work alongside AI researchers, founders, and engineers in Silicon Valley
Stay at the forefront of the latest advances in large language models and AI agents
Help build an AI infrastructure product from 0 to 1
Gain hands-on experience shipping real products used by customers—not just prototypes
Whether you’re a student, researcher, engineer, or aspiring founder, if you’re excited about building the future of AI infrastructure, we’d love to hear from you.
Location
📍 Silicon Valley (Hybrid; some roles can be fully remote)
Apply
📩 Email your resume and a brief introduction to:
paul@bayareafoundersclub.com [ mailto:paul@bayareafoundersclub.com ]
Come build the next generation of AI infrastructure with us—and help create one of the world’s leading AI Gateways.
About the Bay Area Founders Club’s President
About Bay Area Founders Club
Bay Area Founders Club (BFC), founded by Dr. Paul Fang [ https://substack.com/redirect/2056b120-6d5f-4d79-990d-a12d1a8ea0bb?j=eyJ1IjoiN2EzYzFhIn0.gmSjAtdlofMqOFUjgfQgnPyMBlCD_j00mTQEinip6mM ] in Silicon Va

messages.subject: Oak Trees Protecting Our Shoreline in Alameda are in Peril!
messages.to: Carolyn Mason <lpemail62@gmail.com>, Marla Koss ABG <marlakoss@yahoo.com>, Alison Limoges <aslimoges@gmail.com>, Cindy Lacroix <cjlacroix@aol.com>, Birgitt Evans <reachbirgitt@gmail.com>, Tokuda, Jasmine <Jasmine@jasminebazaar.com>
messages.snippet: Good afternoon folks, I've written up the text of my statement to Alameda City Council (See below) and I plan to send a copy via email to each City Council member, so that they have a public record of it. You are welcome to copy my text, ed
messages.text:
Good afternoon folks,
I've written up the text of my statement to Alameda City Council (See
below) and I plan to send a copy via email to each City Council member, so
that they have a public record of it. You are welcome to copy my text, edit
it to your preference, write your own statement or take no action as you
see fit.
Being that I was the main member of CASA advocating for Living Shorelines
and nature-based adaptation to sea-level rise, in our Climate Action
Resilience Plan. I feel implementing the plan in a way that is most likely
to succeed and respects the nature-based adaptation already present and
helping to stabilize the shoreline is important. I realize that my passion
on this subject may not be felt by everyone, and I respect that.
Here's a helpful guide on how to write an email to every City Council
Member.
https://alamedacitizenstaskforce.org/get-involved/how-to-write-to-city-officials/
City Meeting Tues July 21, 2026
From: Damian Mason, member of CASA, OAAC, Alameda Backyard Growers (ABG)
To: Members of Alameda City Council
I’m glad to hear that we may soon have the funds to upgrade our storm drain
infrastructure, implement nature-based shoreline protections and more.  CA
native oak trees of 10 inches or more in diameter are protected in Alameda,
Oakland and other cities in CA.
I’m here on behalf of a small number of young Coastal Live Oaks growing
along the Harbor Bay Shoreline trail between Sea View Park and Harbor Bay
Club. Coastal live oaks stabilize shorelines with deep roots, reducing
erosion. Studies show they outperform hard structures like seawalls,
promoting natural resilience.  This is a key part of nature’s adaptation to
sea level rise. The oak trees in question are only a few years old and 1-3
inch diameter. Unfortunately, the current plan is to remove all of them.
Given their size, they are not yet protected trees, but every small acorn
has the potential to quickly grow into a mighty majestic oak.  I’m
confident we can find a way to incorporate them into the city's
nature-based sea-level rise adaptation plan.
I’ve personally witnessed the dedication Alameda’s city staff and officials
have shown to building Climate Resilience through applied nature-based
design. I trust a path can be found within the shoreline designs to
preserve these trees by incorporating them into the final design plan.
Thank you for your time and attention on this matter.
Damian Mason,
Regenerate, Restore, and Rewild our Ecosystems  #OurCityOurFuture
CASA
